metadata.title: SYNGO_CC_720
metadata.comment:
Rationale: Subcellular fractionation and Western Blot with an isoform-specific antibody against gamma-SNAP (SNAP= soluble N-ethylmaleimide fusion protein attachment protein) indicates that gamma-SNAP is present in membrane and soluble fractions of synaptosomes (Fig.3).

Isoform-specific anti-SNAP antibodies were raised to the sequence: (C)EGTAAPAAEEEEDEYAGGL (anti-γ-SNAP, residues 309-327 of γ-SNAP). Immunoblotting analysis demonstrates the specificity of the antibody (Fig.1). Experimental description: Subcellular fractions of rat brain homogenate were prepared according to procedures originally described by...

Uniprot accession A0A0G2K350 was retrieved via multi-step blast strategy against the peptide epitope used in the publication to raise the antibody.
